request_debug.prompt_preview:
You rerank dead-startup candidates for a slopmortem pipeline. Given a fresh pitch and K candidates retrieved by hybrid search, score each candidate from four perspectives, then return ONLY the top 5 ranked from most to least relevant.

Perspectives (each scored 0-10 with a one-sentence rationale):
- business_model: how similar are the revenue, pricing, and customer-relationship shapes?
- market: how similar are the target buyers, market size, and demand drivers?
- gtm: how similar are the go-to-

response.text: {"ranked":[{"candidate_id":"knewton::edtech","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":7,"rationale":"Knewton licensed a SaaS platform to institutional partners (publishers/educators) on a B2B basis with recurring revenue, closely mirroring the B2B SaaS licensing model of the pitch."},"market":{"score":5,"rationale":"Both target institutional/SMB customers in specialized professional niches, though Knewton focused on education publishers rather than biotech labs or CROs."},"gtm":{"score":6,"rationale":"Both relied on B2B partnership and licensing-led GTM motions targeting organizations rather than direct consumer sales."},"stage_scale":{"score":6,"rationale":"Knewton raised ~$150M and operated at a similar growth-stage SaaS scale before its distressed acquisition, comparable to an early-to-mid-stage SaaS platform failure."}},"rationale":"Knewton is the closest analog as a B2B SaaS platform licensed to institutional partners with subscription-recurring revenue and a partnership-driven GTM, even though the market (edtech vs. biotech) differs."},{"candidate_id":"ubiome::biotech","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":3,"rationale":"uBiome was primarily a DTC and clinical lab-testing service, not a B2B SaaS platform, so revenue and pricing shapes diverge significantly."},"market":{"score":5,"rationale":"Both operate in the biotech/life-sciences sector, though uBiome targeted consumers and insurance payers rather than academic spinouts and CROs."},"gtm":{"score":2,"rationale":"uBiome used DTC crowdfunding and direct clinical channels, which is very different from a B2B SaaS sales motion targeting labs and CROs."},"stage_scale":{"score":5,"rationale":"uBiome raised moderate VC funding and failed at an early-to-mid growth stage, loosely comparable to a seed/Series A SaaS startup."}},"rationale":"Shares the biotech sector and life-sciences domain but diverges substantially on business model, customer type, and GTM; included as the next best biotech match."},{"candidate_id":"theranos::healthtech","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":2,"rationale":"Theranos was a hardware/diagnostics company selling blood-testing devices and services, not a subscription SaaS platform, making the revenue model quite different."},"market":{"score":4,"rationale":"Both touch life-sciences and lab workflows, though Theranos targeted hospitals, pharmacy chains, and consumers rather than academic spinouts and small CROs."},"gtm":{"score":2,"rationale":"Theranos pursued enterprise retail partnerships with Walgreens and Safeway, a very different channel from B2B SaaS licensing to SMB labs."},"stage_scale":{"score":3,"rationale":"Theranos was a heavily funded unicorn at a much larger scale than a typical SMB-focused SaaS startup at failure."}},"rationale":"Shares the lab/diagnostics space in life sciences but diverges on business model, scale, and GTM; only marginally relevant as a sector peer."},{"candidate_id":"parse::devtools","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":5,"rationale":"Parse was a B2B SaaS/PaaS platform with subscription-like usage by developers, making the licensing and platform model somewhat comparable."},"market":{"score":2,"rationale":"Parse targeted mobile app developers broadly, a very different buyer profile from biotech academic spinouts and CROs."},"gtm":{"score":4,"rationale":"Both relied on self-serve and developer/researcher-led adoption with platform licensing, though Parse's channel was app developers rather than lab scientists."},"stage_scale":{"score":5,"rationale":"Parse scaled to 500K apps before shutdown, representing a mid-stage SaaS platform failure at a scale loosely comparable to an early growth-stage biotech SaaS."}},"rationale":"Shares the B2B SaaS platform licensing model and some GTM motion but targets a completely different market; included for business-model shape similarity."},{"candidate_id":"outcome-health::marketing_adtech","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":2,"rationale":"Outcome Health monetized via advertising inventory sold to pharma companies, a fundamentally different model from subscription SaaS licensing."},"market":{"score":3,"rationale":"Both touch healthcare/life-sciences adjacent buyers, but Outcome Health sold to pharma advertisers rather than lab operators or CROs."},"gtm":{"score":2,"rationale":"Outcome Health used a direct enterprise ad-sales motion into pharma, quite different from B2B SaaS licensing to academic spinouts and SMB CROs."},"stage_scale":{"score":3,"rationale":"Outcome Health reached unicorn status at $5.6B valuation, far larger in scale than the SMB-focused early-stage SaaS pitch describes."}},"rationale":"Weakly relevant due to healthcare adjacency and B2B sales motion, but the business model, market, and scale are poor matches; included as the fifth candidate by default."}]}
